qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Fragmented multipart uploads bypass configured file-size limits
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a percent-decoded path traversal in `serve_static` where `string.replace("..", "")` runs before `uri.percent_decode`, enabling arbitrary file read via `%2e%2e`. This finding instead concerns multipart quota accounting in `read_multipart` and `multipart_body`, where fragmented uploads are written before `max_files_size` is decremented.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause in `wisp.serve_static`. The only qualifying finding proves a multipart upload quota-bypass in different code paths and with different security impact.
evidence: The advisory describes `src/wisp.gleam` `serve_static` sanitizing with `string.replace(each: "..", with: "")` before `uri.percent_decode`, which lets percent-encoded `..` survive and become traversal during file reads.
The fixing change moves `uri.percent_decode` before the `".."` stripping step in `serve_static`, confirming the advisory root cause is decode-before-sanitize ordering on static file paths.
The session manifest at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_6f0578ae/.judge/input_manifest.json` lists only one final `qualifying_vuln` finding: `multipart_upload_quota_bypass`.

That finding is grounded in `src/wisp.gleam:1086`, `src/wisp.gleam:1153`, and `src/wisp.gleam:1172`, where multipart body chunks are appended before quota decrementing; its reproduced effect is oversized upload acceptance (`proof accepted bytes=1050000 limit=8`), not arbitrary file read through `serve_static`.
